notes: The traditional meaning of 2.39 — Krishna explicitly names the two doctrines (sāṅkhya and yoga) and pivots from the first to the second; the verse is the structural hinge that authorizes the chapter's three-thread reading; the discipline named here, when joined with wisdom, casts off the bondage of action — is preserved exactly. The engineering layer's META structure honors the verse's META structure: it does not turn 2.39 into another argued position but treats it as the act of naming. The 'karma-bandham prahāsyasi' is rendered (following Shankara) as the engineering form of bondage — work that produces more work without reducing the underlying problem. The directional pressure of the verse (toward the second discipline as the new topic) is preserved through the explicit forward-pointing of the engineering layer ('Krishna's pivot here gives the engineer permission — and the structural mandate — to name both as separate disciplines and to develop both' — note this was in v0; the v1 trim preserves the same content tightened). No inversion. PASS.
